descriptions: Keiji Union split from Tankai-ikka.
edges.evidence_quote: the Keiji Union, which had split from the 6th generation Yamaguchi-gumi-affiliated Tankai-ikka

descriptions: Keiji Union joined the Ninkyo-dantai-Yamaguchi-gumi (later Kizuna-kai).
edges.evidence_quote: Ikeda and others attended the formation ceremony of the Keiji Union, which had split from the 6th generation Yamaguchi-gumi-affiliated Tankai-ikka, and it is believed that this group also joined the Ninkyo-dantai-Yamaguchi-gumi.

Bamboo Union.description: The Bamboo Union is the largest triad in Taiwan, founded in 1957 by waishengren children in Taipei. It has roughly 20,000 members and is involved in drug trafficking, contract killing, and extortion. The gang has historic ties to the Kuomintang and operates internationally in the United States, China, and other countries.

descriptions: Bamboo Union and Celestial Way both attended Chen Chi-li's funeral, indicating a non-hostile relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Representatives of Taiwan's three main criminal organizations, the Bamboo Union, the Four Seas Triad and the Celestial Way, were on hand to pay their respects to Mr. Li.

descriptions: Bamboo Union and Four Seas Triad both attended Chen Chi-li's funeral, indicating a non-hostile relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Representatives of Taiwan's three main criminal organizations, the Bamboo Union, the Four Seas Triad and the Celestial Way, were on hand to pay their respects to Mr. Li.

descriptions: Bamboo Union cooperated with Iron Blood Patriots for overseas missions and heroin trade.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1980s, the gang became even more powerful when they joined hands with the "Iron Blood Patriots" to carry out missions overseas.

descriptions: Bamboo Union partners with Sam Gor syndicate for drug trafficking and money laundering.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bamboo Union is understood to partner with the Japanese yakuza and multi-ethnic triads like Sam Gor syndicate

descriptions: Yakuza members attended Chen Chi-li's funeral, and the text states Bamboo Union partners with yakuza.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of Japan's yakuza, dressed in black suits, were also among the guests

descriptions: Bamboo Union is a constituent triad of Sam Gor.
edges.evidence_quote: Sam Gor is made up of 14K, Wo Shing Wo, Sun Yee On, Big Circle Gang and Bamboo Union.

descriptions: Bamboo Union is a triad based in Taiwan.
edges.evidence_quote: Based elsewhere: - Bamboo Union , Taiwan

descriptions: Bamboo Union is Taiwan's largest Triad; Chen Chi-li is a former leader.
edges.evidence_quote: Chen Chi-li , former leader of Bamboo Union , Taiwan's largest Triad

descriptions: Bamboo Union is listed as a rival of Wah Ching in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bamboo Union

Sureños.description: Sureños are loosely affiliated gangs that pay tribute to the Mexican Mafia while in U.S. correctional facilities. Founded in 1967 in Southern California, they are primarily Mexican American and engage in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and other crimes. They have a long-standing rivalry with Norteños and Nuestra Familia. The gang operates in 35 U.S. states and remains active.

descriptions: Sureños (US) is an ally of 18th Street.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia, Sureños, 38th Street gang

descriptions: Sureños are the remaining faction of 18th Street after the split, rivals of Revolucionarios.
edges.evidence_quote: a faction called the "Revolucionarios ("Revolutionaries") split off 18th Street in 2005, becoming rivals with the other members, who came to be known as the Sureños ("Southerners.")

descriptions: Sureños is listed as an ally of 38th Street Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia , Sureños , Street Saints 13 , 18th Street gang , Lennox 13

descriptions: Armenian Power had conflict with Sureño gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Armenian Power has had a history of conflict with Sureño gangs, which are controlled by the Mexican Mafia, in the past, but the war is thought to have ended in recent years.

descriptions: Armenian Power now works together with Sureños.
edges.evidence_quote: Today the Mexican Mafia prison gang collects protection money from Armenian Power, which has added the number 13 to its name as a sign of loyalty. The two gangs, and the Sureños, now work together to both control and extort protection money from criminal activities in the Los Angeles area.

descriptions: Infobox lista a algunas cuadrillas de los Sureños como aliadas.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Algunas cuadrillas de los Sureños

descriptions: Sureños is an ally of Aryan Brotherhood per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dirty White Boys, Gambino crime family, Hells Angels, Mexican Mafia, Nazi Lowriders, Public Enemy No. 1, Sureños, Vagos MC

descriptions: Sureños are vassal gangs controlled by the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican Mafia is the controlling organization for almost every Hispanic gang in Southern California, and some gangs located in Central and Northern California, with their vassal gangs being called Sureños.

descriptions: Mexican Mafia orders Sureños to collect taxes.

descriptions: Sureños were used by Mexican Mafia to threaten Mongols, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the Mexican Mafia requested payment from the club. In March 2004, Mexican Mafia "shot callers" sent out the message that the Sureños had sustained over $20,000 in loss of drug revenue and personal damages and ordered the Mongols to pay for the lost revenue, threatening to put the club's members on the "green light" list, making them a target for murder by the Sureños in and out of custody.

descriptions: Mongols are allied with Sureños.
edges.evidence_quote: The club is allied with the Mexican Mafia – Sureño coalition, the Nuestra Familia – Norteño alliance, the Nazi Lowriders, and the Tijuana Cartel.

Waray-Waray gangs.description: Generic term for criminal groups of Waray ethnicity in the Philippines, originating from Leyte and Samar. They engage in armed robbery, kidnapping, carnapping, gun running, assassinations, gang warfare, and narcotics sales. Known for bloody confrontations with police, they are considered the most notorious gangs in the Philippines.

descriptions: Waray-Ozamiz Gang is an alliance between Waray and Ozamiz criminals, thus a sub-group of Waray-Waray gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: This group is unique because it is basically an alliance between Waray and Ozamiz criminals, hence the name.

descriptions: Sudanese Awakening Revolutionary Council is listed as an opponent of RSF.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Sudanese Awakening Revolutionary Council

descriptions: Hezbollah opposed the March 14 Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Although Hezbollah had joined the new government in 2005, it remained staunchly opposed to the March 14 Alliance.

'Ndrina Perna.description: The 'Ndrina Perna is a 'ndrina (criminal clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. It emerged after the death of boss Luigi Palermo in 1977, splitting into two factions and engaging in a violent feud with the Pino-Sena clan that resulted in dozens of murders. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking and other criminal activities, with several police operations targeting its members.

descriptions: The Bruni and Perna groups are both mentioned in the same investigation about corrupt prison guards.
edges.evidence_quote: Grazie ai collaboratori Ernesto e Adolfo Foggetti e Mattia Pulicanò si è scoperto che due agenti corrotti avrebbero fornito ad esponenti degli zingari Rango e i Lanzino-Patitucci in carcere telefoni cellulari, droga e messaggi. I fatti sarebbero risalenti agli anni 2013 e 2014. Nell'inchiesta vengono citati anche i Bruni e i Perna.

descriptions: The 'Ndrina Perna is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Perna è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese di Cosenza.

descriptions: Africano allied with Perna.
edges.evidence_quote: Con i Perna si allearono anche gli Africano di Amantea

descriptions: A war broke out between the two factions.
edges.evidence_quote: Scoppia subito dopo una guerra tra le due famiglie.

descriptions: The 'Ndrina Perna split into two factions: Perna-Pranno-Vitelli and Pino-Sena.
edges.evidence_quote: la 'ndrina si divise in due fazioni, da un lato i Perna-Pranno-Vitelli e dall'altra i Pino-Sena.

edges.evidence_quote: Alla fine degli anni Settanta si schierarono con i Pino-Sena in faida con i Perna-Pranno-Vitelli.

Kyushu Seido-kai.description: The Kyushu Seido-kai was a yakuza organization based in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan, formed in 2006 as a splinter group from the Dojin-kai. It engaged in violent armed conflicts with its former parent syndicate, using automatic firearms and grenades, and was involved in drug trafficking and loansharking. The group was designated as a yakuza group in 2008 and dissolved in June 2013, later reorganizing under new names.

descriptions: Dojin-kai and its splinter group Kyushu Seido-kai have been in a violent conflict since 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai again started a blatantly vicious conflict with its splinter group, Kyushu Seido-kai, in 2006.

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Namikawa-kai are rivals per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: The Namikawa-kai and the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai engaged in armed conflicts with its former parent syndicate Dojin-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Since its formation, the Kyushu Seido-kai has been known for its blatant armed conflicts with its former parent syndicate, the Dojin-kai

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai ended its gang war with Dojin-kai and dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: On June 11, 2013, Kyushu Seido-kai announced the end of its gang war with Dojin-kai as well as its dissolution.

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai was absent from Kudo-kai's president's funeral, indicating no close ties.
edges.evidence_quote: The Seido-kai was the only designated yakuza syndicate absent from this event.

descriptions: Namikawa-kai is a designated yakuza group, a subset of Yakuza.
edges.evidence_quote: Fukuoka Prefecture has the largest number of designated yakuza groups among all of the prefectures, at 5; the Kudo-kai , the Taishu-kai , the Fukuhaku-kai , the Dojin-kai , and the Namikawa-kai .

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai aligned with Yamaguchi-gumi against Dojin-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: They allegedly aligned themselves with the Yamaguchi-gumi, the Dojin-kai's rival and the largest yakuza syndicate in Japan.

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai's president maintained a close relationship with Yamaken-gumi's president.
edges.evidence_quote: The second president Namikawa has allegedly maintained a close relationship with Kunio Inoue. Inoue is the president of the fourth-generation Yamaken-gumi

descriptions: Okaida is listed as a rival of PCC in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Comando Vermelho , Família do Norte , Militias , Okaida [ pt ] , Bala na Cara [ pt ] , Primeiro Grupo Catarinense [ pt ] , Sindicato do Crime [ pt ] , Clan Rotela

descriptions: Okaida is listed as a rival of PCC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Comando Vermelho , Família do Norte , Militias , Okaida [ pt ] , Bala na Cara [ pt ] , Primeiro Grupo Catarinense [ pt ] , Sindicato do Crime [ pt ] , Clan Rotela

descriptions: PCC lists Okaida as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Comando Vermelho , Família do Norte , Militias , Okaida [ pt ] , Bala na Cara [ pt ] , Primeiro Grupo Catarinense [ pt ] , Sindicato do Crime [ pt ] , Clan Rotela

White Hand Gang.description: The White Hand Gang was a collection of Irish American gangs on the New York City, Brooklyn, and Red Hook waterfronts from the early 1900s to 1925. They organized against the growing influence of Italian gangsters, choosing their name in response to the Sicilian Black Hand gangs. The gang was virulently anti-Italian and particularly violent, with unstable leadership contributing to its demise.

descriptions: Frankie Yale's Mafia family waged war with the White Hand Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In New York City, Frankie Yale waged war with the Irish-American White Hand Gang.

descriptions: The American Mafia (via Frankie Yale) fought with the White Hand Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: En Nueva York, Frankie Yale combatió con los irlandeses estadounidense de la White Hand Gang .

descriptions: Rival gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: White Hand Gang

descriptions: Swamp Angels merged with rival waterfront gangs to form the White Hand Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: until eventually they merged with the rival waterfront gangs into the White Hand Gang at the end of the 19th century.

descriptions: White Hand Gang named itself in opposition to Black Hand gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Their name was chosen in response to the Sicilian Black Hand gangs

descriptions: White Hand Gang was rival to Italian gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Hand gangs , Italian gangsters

descriptions: The White Hand Gang formed to keep Italian rivals like the Morello crime family at bay.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1900s, with Italian criminal organizations such as the Morello crime family encroaching on the waterfront, various Irish gangs united to form the White Hand Gang.

descriptions: HXP is listed as an ally of SDF in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Allies Germany , United States [ a ] , United Kingdom [ b ] , United Arab Emirates (2017–2018) [ c ] , Saudi Arabia (2018) [ d ] , Kurdistan Region , Patriotic Union of Kurdistan , Kurdistan Workers' Party (until 2025) , International Freedom Battalion , France , Ba'athist Syria (sometimes, until 2024) [ e ] , Iraq (sometimes) Popular Mobilization Forces (sometimes) Liwa al-Tufuf , Iran (sometimes) , Russia (sometimes) [ f ] , Some groups in the Syrian opposition , NES police forces Northern Syria Internal Security Forces Asayish Anti-Terror Forces (HAT SWAT units) , Manbij Internal Security Forces Quick Reaction Force unit , Raqqa Internal Security Forces Quick Reaction Force unit , Deir ez-Zor Internal Security Forces Quick Reaction Force unit , Sutoro Bethnahrain Women's Protection Forces police branch , NES civilian defence forces Self-Defense Forces (HXP) Special Forces , Syrian Border Security Force (BSF) , Civilian Defense Force (HPC)

descriptions: OutLaw Gangster Disciples is allied with Zoe Pound Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Folk Nation , Crips , Zoe Pound Gang , Black Guerilla Family , and Black Mafia Family

Corleone Mafia family.description: The Corleone Mafia family is a crime family of Cosa Nostra based in Corleone, Sicily. Founded around the early 1900s by Angelo Gagliano, it rose to become the most powerful mafia family in Italy and the world under Totò Riina in the 1980s. After the Second Mafia War, it dominated Cosa Nostra until repression in the early 1990s led to many members' imprisonment. Today it remains active with about 70 members, many incarcerated.

descriptions: Luciano Leggio, boss of the Corleone family, linked himself to 'Ndrangheta figures.
edges.evidence_quote: Here he also linked himself to figures of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta, such as Domenico "Mico" Tripodo and his deputy Paolo De Stefano.

descriptions: The Badalamenti family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.

descriptions: The Bontade family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.

descriptions: Lorenzo Nuvoletta, a Camorra boss, was an associate of Leggio and his clan was also a branch of Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Lorenzo Nuvoletta , who was the head of a mafia family operating in the Naples area, as a Camorra clan, but which was also a local branch of Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Bernardino Verro, associated with the Fasci Siciliani, joined the Corleone family for protection.
edges.evidence_quote: Verro, a trade union leader associated with the Fasci Siciliani and former mayor of Corleone, had himself joined the Corleone Mafia family, known among its members as the Fratuzzi (“Little Brothers”), in order to strengthen the strikes and ensure his personal protection.

descriptions: The Inzerillo family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.

descriptions: Angelo Gagliano, founder of the Corleone family, was a relative of Tommy Gagliano, founder of the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Angelo Gagliano, a relative of Tommy Gagliano , who would later establish the Lucchese crime family in New York City .

descriptions: The Corleone family supplanted the Palermo families after winning the Second Mafia War.
edges.evidence_quote: growing to the point of supplanting the Palermo families in the 1980s, when it emerged victorious from the Second Mafia War (1981–1982).

descriptions: The Corleone family is a constituent crime family of Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleone Mafia family , also called the Leggio Mafia family , is a crime family of the Cosa Nostra based in the town of Corleone , Sicily .

descriptions: The Corleone Mafia family is a core component of the Corleonesi alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable leaders included Luciano Leggio, Salvatore Riina, Bernardo Provenzano, and Leoluca Bagarella from the Corleone Mafia family

descriptions: Ramacca Mafia family is allied with Corleone Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family , Caltagirone Mafia family , Corleone Mafia family

descriptions: Deutsche Eiche is one of the clubs that joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The criminal clubs of the 1920s had poetic names such as "Berliner", "Immertreu" ("ever faithful"), "Libelle" ("Dragonfly"), "Deutsche Eiche" (German oak), or "Apachenblut" ("blood of the Apache").

descriptions: Both groups operated in Seoul during the 1950s with a similar purpose.
edges.evidence_quote: two separate Seoul-based groups, the Myung-dong and the Chong-ro, operated to protect Korean merchants from Japanese criminals

descriptions: Keiji Union split from Tankai-ikka.
edges.evidence_quote: the Keiji Union, which had split from the 6th generation Yamaguchi-gumi-affiliated Tankai-ikka

Los Tiguerones.description: Ecuadorian narcoterrorist and criminal organization specializing in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. It split from Los Choneros in 2021 after the assassination of their leader. Based in Esmeraldas, with operations in Guayaquil and Manta. Led by William Jofre Alcívar Bautista, a former prison guard. Gained notoriety for the armed takeover of TC Televisión in January 2024.

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Tiguerones as allies of Los Chone Killers.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Los Tiguerones, Los Lobos

descriptions: Los Tiguerones is listed as a rival of Los Choneros in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Lagartos, Los Lobos, Los Tiguerones, Los Chone Killers

descriptions: Los Tiguerones were formerly part of Los Choneros but later became rivals, fighting for territory.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Tiguerones ... que funcionaban como parte de Los Choneros ... se unieron en una nueva estructura ... para disputar el territorio a Los Choneros

descriptions: Los Lobos list Los Tiguerones as enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Los Choneros, Los Tiguerones, Los Chone Killers, Latin Kings

descriptions: Los Lobos and Los Tiguerones cooperated in a wave of violence.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos, along with their ally Los Tiguerones, were behind a wave of violence across Ecuador

descriptions: Los Lobos and Los Tiguerones united to form Nueva Generación.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos , Los Tiguerones , Los Chone Killers y Los Pipos que funcionaban como parte de Los Choneros y protagonizaron la masacre carcelaria, se unieron en una nueva estructura en 2021, la cual ha sido denominada Nueva Generación

descriptions: Los Lobos and Los Tiguerones acted together in a wave of violence.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos, juntamente com o seu aliado Los Tiguerones, estiveram por trás de uma onda de violência em todo o Equador

descriptions: Los Lobos split from Los Choneros at the same time as Los Tiguerones.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos comenzaron como un grupo escondido del cártel de drogas Los Choneros, pero se separaron en 2020 después de la muerte de Jorge Luis Zambrano, junto con Los Chone Killers y Los Tiguerones.

descriptions: Los Lobos and Los Tiguerones both dissented from Los Choneros at the same time.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos começou como um grupo dissidente do cartel de drogas Los Choneros, mas se separou em 2020 após a morte de Jorge Luis Zambrano, juntamente com Los Chone Killers e Los Tiguerones

descriptions: Los Tiguerones are allied with the Albanian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación , Mafia albanesa Los del norte

descriptions: Los Tiguerones formed an alliance with Jalisco New Generation Cartel called Nueva Generación.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2021, Los Tiguerones, along with other gangs formerly allied to Los Choneros, including Los Chone Killers, Los Pipos, and Los Lobos, announced the creation of an alliance dubbed Nueva Generación after an alliance with Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

descriptions: Los Tiguerones are allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación , Mafia albanesa Los del norte

descriptions: Kompania Bello is listed as an ally of Los Tiguerones in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Los Chone Killers, Los Lobos, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Kompania Bello

descriptions: Los Tiguerones and Los Chone Killers are allies in the Nueva Generación alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2021, Los Tiguerones, along with other gangs formerly allied to Los Choneros, including Los Chone Killers, Los Pipos, and Los Lobos, announced the creation of an alliance dubbed Nueva Generación after an alliance with Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

descriptions: Los Tiguerones and Los Chone Killers united to form Nueva Generación.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos , Los Tiguerones , Los Chone Killers y Los Pipos ... se unieron en una nueva estructura en 2021, la cual ha sido denominada Nueva Generación

descriptions: Los Tiguerones and Los Chone Killers both split from Los Choneros at the same time.
edges.evidence_quote: El grupo se dividió de Los Choneros en 2021, junto con Los Lobos y Los Chone Killers tras el asesinato del líder de Los Choneros, Jorge Luís Zambrano alias 'Rasquiña'.

descriptions: Los Choneros are listed as rivals of Los Tiguerones in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Choneros

descriptions: Los Tiguerones consider Los Choneros enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Los Choneros Los Lobos

descriptions: Los Tiguerones split from Los Choneros in 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: The group split from Los Choneros in 2020, along with Los Lobos and Los Chone Killers following the murder of Los Choneros leader Jorge Luis Zambrano.

descriptions: Los Tiguerones split from Los Choneros in 2021.
edges.evidence_quote: El grupo se dividió de Los Choneros en 2021, junto con Los Lobos y Los Chone Killers tras el asesinato del líder de Los Choneros, Jorge Luís Zambrano alias 'Rasquiña'.

descriptions: Los Tiguerones are allied with Los del norte.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación , Mafia albanesa Los del norte

descriptions: Los Tiguerones consider Los Lobos enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Los Choneros Los Lobos

descriptions: Los Tiguerones and Los Lobos are allies in the Nueva Generación alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2021, Los Tiguerones, along with other gangs formerly allied to Los Choneros, including Los Chone Killers, Los Pipos, and Los Lobos, announced the creation of an alliance dubbed Nueva Generación after an alliance with Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

descriptions: Los Tiguerones and Los Lobos both split from Los Choneros at the same time.
edges.evidence_quote: El grupo se dividió de Los Choneros en 2021, junto con Los Lobos y Los Chone Killers tras el asesinato del líder de Los Choneros, Jorge Luís Zambrano alias 'Rasquiña'.

descriptions: Los Tiguerones and Los Pipos are allies in the Nueva Generación alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2021, Los Tiguerones, along with other gangs formerly allied to Los Choneros, including Los Chone Killers, Los Pipos, and Los Lobos, announced the creation of an alliance dubbed Nueva Generación after an alliance with Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

descriptions: Los Tiguerones and Los Pipos united to form Nueva Generación.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos , Los Tiguerones , Los Chone Killers y Los Pipos ... se unieron en una nueva estructura en 2021, la cual ha sido denominada Nueva Generación

descriptions: Tren de Aragua was a rival of Los Tiguerones until November 2023.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Los Tiguerones (until November 2023)

descriptions: Tren de Aragua lists Los Tiguerones as a rival until November 2023.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: La Oficina, Clan del Golfo, FARC dissidents, ELN, Los Tiguerones (until November 2023), Latin Kings [ unreliable source? ], La Unión Tepito (certain cells), Fuerza Anti-Unión, Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Listed as enemy in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Los Tiguerones

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Tiguerones as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Los Tiguerones

descriptions: Los Tiguerones is listed as an enemy of Tren de Aragua in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Clan del Golfo , Oficina de Envigado , Disidencias de las FARC-EP , Cartel de Sinaloa , Los Tiguerones , Latin Kings , Fuerza Anti-Unión , Comando Vermelho , Mara Barrio 18 , Anti-Tren De Aragua

descriptions: Tren de Aragua lists Los Tiguerones as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Los Tiguerones

descriptions: Tren de Aragua is allied with Los Tiguerones since November 2023.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Los Tiguerones (from November 2023)

descriptions: Tren de Aragua lists Los Tiguerones as an ally from November 2023.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Venezuela (via the Cartel of the Suns, alleged by the US government), Primeiro Comando da Capital, Comando Vermelho, La Empresa, La Linea, La Unión Tepito, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Los Tiguerones (from November 2023), Los Lobos

descriptions: Gente Nueva (Sinaloa Cartel) had a dispute with the Beltrán Leyva Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: tenía una disputa contra la Organización Beltrán Leyva , La Línea , y Los Zetas

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel allied with Los Beltrán Leyva.
edges.evidence_quote: el Chapo Guzmán acuerda una alianza con otros capos, entre ellos Nazario Moreno González , Vicente Carrillo Fuentes , Los Beltrán Leyva y Los Valencia ( Cartel del Milenio )

descriptions: The Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family is listed as an ally of the Catania family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: , Pietraperzia Mafia family , Caltagirone Mafia family , Ramacca Mafia family , Riesi Mafia family , Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family , Passo di Rigano Mafia family , Casalesi clan , Licciardi clan , Mallardo clan , De Stefano 'ndrina

descriptions: The Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family

descriptions: Riina distributed extortion money to Santa Maria di Gesù family.
edges.evidence_quote: metà spettava a Santa Maria di Gesù (quindi ai fratelli Ignazio e Giovanbattista Pullarà e poi a Pietro Aglieri)

descriptions: The Corleonesi waged war against Bontade's coalition, which included the Santa Maria di Gesù Family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi initiated the war against the coalition led by Bontade and Badalamenti to try to control heroin trafficking.

descriptions: The Inzerillo clan lists the Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family

descriptions: Bontade of the Santa Maria di Gesù Family cooperated with the Camorra clan Maisto.
edges.evidence_quote: Bontade, for instance, linked up with Giuseppe Sciorio of the Maisto clan of the Camorra, who would be initiated in Cosa Nostra.

descriptions: The Corleonesi killed Bontade, the boss of the Santa Maria di Gesù Family, indicating conflict between the families.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, he was killed by the rival faction within Cosa Nostra, the Corleonesi.

descriptions: The Santa Maria di Gesù Family (via Bontade's network) supplied heroin to the Gambino Family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bontade-Spatola-Inzerillo traffickers supplied the Gambino Family – through John Gambino – in New York with heroin.

descriptions: Bontade was an ally of the Greco clan of Ciaculli.
edges.evidence_quote: Historically, the Greco clan from Croceverde Giardini had been at odds with the Greco clan of Ciaculli led by Salvatore 'Ciaschiteddu' Greco, of whom Bontade was an ally.

descriptions: The Santa Maria di Gesù Family is a constituent family of the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: He was the boss of the Santa Maria di Gesù Family in Palermo.

descriptions: Bontade's family was part of the Spatola-Inzerillo-Gambino network.
edges.evidence_quote: Bontade was closely linked to the Spatola-Inzerillo-Gambino network.

descriptions: Barrio Azteca is a rival of Border Brothers.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Mexicles , Sinaloa Cartel , Border Brothers

descriptions: Barrio Azteca has Border Brothers as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Mexicles , Sinaloa Cartel , Border Brothers

descriptions: Border Brothers are listed as rivals of Norteños in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Fresno Bulldogs , Sureños , MS-13 , Mexican Mafia , Aryan Brotherhood , 18th Street gang , White Fence , Border Brothers, Tiny Rascal Gang , Oriental Troopz, Mongols MC

descriptions: Sureños list Border Brothers as enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Border Brothers

descriptions: The attack on Trisciuoglio, Prencipe, and Piserchia was an attempt by a rival clan to eliminate the winning battery, referring to the Pellegrino group.
edges.evidence_quote: È il tentativo del clan rivale di spazzare i vertici della «batteria» vincente in quel momento.

descriptions: The Mercante faction fought a war against the Telegrafo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Un'altra guerra in cui il clan è stato coinvolto, ma questa volta guidata dai Mercante, fu contro il clan Telegrafo.

'Ndrina Forastefano.description: The 'Ndrina Forastefano is a criminal clan of the 'Ndrangheta operating in the Sibaritide area of Calabria, Italy. It is involved in extortion, usury, vote buying, drug trafficking, fraud, human smuggling, and infiltration of public contracts and local economic sectors. Originally united with the Abbruzzese clan, it emerged victorious from a feud against the Zingari of Lauropoli in the early 2000s, gaining control over criminal activities in the upper Ionian coast of Cosenza.

descriptions: The Abbruzzese and Forastefano were both targeted in the same police operation, indicating a connection.
edges.evidence_quote: 16 febbraio 2021: operazione Kossa contro i Forastefano e gli Abbruzzese.

descriptions: Faillace fought alongside Forastefano in the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: insieme ai Forastefano e i Portoraro

descriptions: Forastefano is a subunit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Forastefano è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: Forastefano and Abbruzzese were originally one single 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: In origine era un'unica 'ndrina insieme agli Abbruzzese

descriptions: Federico Faillace was an affiliate of Forastefano and also head of Faillace-Portoraro, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 22 agosto 2009 viene ucciso ... Federico Faillace ... presunto affiliato ai Forastefano, nonché capo dei Faillace-Portoraro

descriptions: Forastefano was involved in a feud against the Zingari di Lauropoli.
edges.evidence_quote: È stata coinvolta alla fine degli anni novanta e all'inizio del nuovo secolo nella faida contro gli Zingari di Lauropoli

descriptions: The two gangs are described as rivalling each other.
edges.evidence_quote: There are many widespread accusations that the government has been protecting two rivalling gangs, GRIB Jaya and Pancasila Youth for political and economic interests.

United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia.description: Colombian far-right paramilitary and drug trafficking group active from 1997 to 2006. Formed through a merger of local right-wing militias to combat left-wing guerrillas. Heavily financed by drug trade and local elites, with alleged ties to Colombian military. Responsible for numerous massacres and human rights abuses. Demobilized by 2006, but successor groups remain.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta dealt directly with AUC in cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Quando si inserisce nel traffico di cocaina negli anni ottanta e comincia a controllarne i traffici si sposta in Spagna , Portogallo , Paesi Bassi fino ad arrivare in Colombia e a trattare direttamente con i Narcos e con l' AUC di Salvatore Mancuso Gómez .

descriptions: Mancuso 'ndrina historically had contacts with AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: historically with the ... United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Bloque Meta originated from former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Bloque Meta's history can be traced back to the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia, or AUC.

descriptions: Cartel de La Guajira supplied weapons to the Bloque Norte de las AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: El cartel también trafica con armas en el mercado negro, siendo proveedor del extinto del grupo narcoparamilitar Bloque Norte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Norte del Valle Cartel employed AUC for protection.
edges.evidence_quote: the cartel members employed the services of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a right-wing paramilitary organization internationally classified as terrorist, to protect the cartel's drug routes, its drug laboratories, and its members and associates.

descriptions: Leaders of Los Pepes later led the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: several of their leaders eventually went on to become leaders of a national paramilitary alliance in Colombia, the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Los Rastrojos members conspired with AUC commander Carlos Mario Jiménez to murder Wilber Varela.
edges.evidence_quote: Authorities now believe Wilber Varela's main captains Diego Perez Henao alias 'Diego Rastrojo' and Luis Enrique Calle alias 'Combatiente' ('Combatant') conspired with AUC commander Carlos Mario Jiménez alias 'Macaco' to murder Varela.

descriptions: AUC thwarted ELN's demilitarized zone initiative and conducts anti-guerrilla operations against ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: A Colombian government initiative towards granting the ELN a demilitarized zone in the southern region of the Bolívar Department was thwarted by right-wing political pressure from the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC) whose paramilitary mercenaries conduct anti-guerrilla operations in that part of the department.

descriptions: North Coast Cartel hired AUC for protection of drug routes and labs.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel is believed to have employed the services of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a right-wing paramilitary organization internationally classified as a terrorist organization, to protect the cartel's drug routes, its drug laboratories, and its members and associates.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado affiliated with AUC and organized drug trafficking for them.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization then inherited the Medellín turf and its criminal connections in the US, Mexico, and the UK, and began to affiliate with the paramilitary United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia, organizing drug trafficking operations on their behalf.

descriptions: Oficina affiliated with AUC, organizing drug trafficking operations for them.
edges.evidence_quote: Murillo then began to affiliate his Oficina with the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU was a founding component of the AUC, with Carlos Castaño leading both.
edges.evidence_quote: After Fidel Castaño's disappearance, his brother Carlos took over the ACCU and eventually led it to the formation of the United Self-Defenders of Colombia (AUC), which grouped other small paramilitary armies organized throughout Colombia.

descriptions: AUC expressed support for anti-FARC protests, indicating opposition to FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: Shortly before the rallies took place 13 demobilised AUC paramilitary leaders, including Salvatore Mancuso, had expressed their support of the protest through a communique. However, this move was rejected by organiser Carlos Andrés Santiago, who stated that such an endorsement was harmful and criticised the AUC's actions.

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel established contacts with AUC for cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: apoderándose del tráfico de cocaína colombiana en los mercados mundiales gracias a los contactos establecidos con la guerrilla de las FARC y los paramilitares de las AUC

descriptions: AUC cooperated with 'Ndrangheta in cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the leadership of Salvatore Mancuso, son of Italian immigrants, the AUC maintained close links with the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta concerning cocaine trafficking.

edges.target: Bandas y grupos emergentes en Colombia
descriptions: BACRIM is a successor organization to the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC was supposed to have demobilized completely by 2006 but successor organizations continued to operate such as the neo-paramilitary alliance BACRIM ('bandas criminales emergentes').

descriptions: AUC considered ELN as main enemy and engaged in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC's main enemies were the leftist insurgency groups Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) and the National Liberation Army (ELN).

descriptions: AUC retaliated against and attacked ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC was responsible for retaliations against the FARC and ELN communist organization as well as numerous attacks against civilians beginning in 1997 with the Mapiripán massacre.

descriptions: Both AUC and ELN are involved in drug trafficking in Colombia, but no direct relationship is stated.
